title: Project Card v1
description: The project anchor: definition, purpose, scope, status, stakeholders, and (optionally) the operational layer — timeline, budget, environments, references. One per project folder as project.json. This is a SUPERSET of the meeting-coach project-card: the core fields are identical, so a meeting-coach companion reads the same file; the operational blocks are optional additions that project-assistant uses to run the work.

id.description: Format: {customer_code}-{project_code}. No user prefix — the project-card is a shared artifact. Examples: ACME-CLOUD, STOWLINE-ETL, NRTH-PORTAL.

purpose.description: 1-3 sentences: why are we doing this? What does it solve? Hard non-empty — a mechanism against empty metadata. It keeps you from forgetting why the project exists.

out_of_scope.description: Bullets: what is explicitly out of scope. May be empty.

success_criteria.description: Measurable statements at the project level (NOT at action-card level). Required once status is execution, golive, aftercare, or done.

status.description: Lifecycle phase. Health (green/yellow/red) is a separate signal, not a status.

since.description: Since when at this level (duration signal).

code.description: Short code used in the project id.

project_lead.description: Optional reference to a contact-card; may be null when there is no single point of contact.

customer_project_id.description: The customer's own project number, if any.

stakeholders.description: Key people across customer, vendor, and internal sides. When contact_id is set, the contact-card is the authoritative source — name/role here are convenience copies. This is the bridge field shared with meeting-coach.

role.description: Role on the project, e.g. 'project sponsor', 'tech lead', 'vendor PM'.

budget.description: Optional operational block. Omit for projects you do not track financially.

environments.description: Optional operational block. Environments overview (dev/test/acc/prod, URLs, status). May be empty.

third_parties.description: Optional: other organizations involved (not you, not the customer).

risks.description: Short list of top project risks. May be empty.

classification.description: Optional free-form size/track label from your own method, e.g. 'S', 'M/internal', 'L'.

reporting.description: Steering/stakeholder reporting cadence for this project. Astrid uses it to flag when a project is overdue for a report and to scope 'what changed since the last report'.

cadence.description: How often this project is reported on. 'ad-hoc'/'none' carry no automatic due-date.

audience.description: Who the report goes to, e.g. 'steering group', 'sponsor'.

last_reported.description: Date the last report was issued. Astrid updates this when it produces a steering report.

next_due.description: Optional manual override of the next report date; otherwise derived from cadence + last_reported by the tooling.

action_cards_glob.description: Glob pattern to find this project's action-cards, e.g. 'cards/AB-ACME-CLOUD-*.json'.

meetings_glob.description: Glob pattern to find this project's meeting-cards, e.g. 'meetings/MTG-ACME-CLOUD-*.json'.
